title: Homework5
text:
The purpose of lapply is to apply a function to each element of the supplied list. The equivalent purr function is map
lapply(my_list, FUN = cor, method = "kendall")
purr functions work within the tidyverse (they are pipeable) as well as having consistent arguments across different types that we are working with
A side-effect function is one that performs a certain task outside of the code (plot, print, generate csv, etc) and leaves the original data as-is
sd is defined locally within the function, so if that function is being called, R looks within that function’s namespace first. If R does not find a variable called sd() with a function, it will use whatever is defined globally.







getRMSE = function(y, yHat, ...){
  
  devs = (y - yHat)^2
  RMSE = sqrt(mean(devs, ...))
  
  return(RMSE)
  
}




Run example code

set.seed(10)
n <- 100
x <- runif(n)
resp <- 3 + 10 * x + rnorm(n)
pred <- predict(lm(resp ~ x), data.frame(x))

Test

getRMSE(y = resp, yHat = pred)

[1] 0.9581677


Test without specifying NA behavior

resp[c(23,15)] = NA_real_

## Without NA Behavior
getRMSE(y = resp, yHat = pred)

[1] NA


Test With NA Behavior

resp[c(23,15)] = NA_real_

## Without NA Behavior
getRMSE(y = resp, yHat = pred, na.rm = TRUE)

[1] 0.9537965






getMAE = function(y, yHat, ...){
  absDev = abs(y - yHat)
  
  MAE = (mean(absDev, ...))
  return(MAE)
}





set.seed(10)
n <- 100
x <- runif(n)
resp <- 3 + 10 * x + rnorm(n)
pred <- predict(lm(resp ~ x), data.frame(x))

Test without removing variables

getMAE(y = resp, yHat = pred)

[1] 0.8155776


Remove some responses:

resp[c(7,11)] = NA_real_

Test without NA behavior

getMAE(y = resp, yHat = pred)

[1] NA


Test with NA behavior

getMAE(y = resp, yHat = pred, na.rm = TRUE)

[1] 0.8092874






getErrors = function(y, yHat, output = "all", ...){
  

    if (is.atomic(y) & is.numeric(y) & is.atomic(y)){
      invisible(y)
    }
    else{
      stop("Bad input")
      
    if (is.atomic(yHat) & is.numeric(yHat) & is.atomic(yHat)){
      invisible(y)
    }
    else{
      stop("Bad input")}
    
    
    
    
  }
  
  if (output == "RMSE"){
    return(c("RMSE" = getRMSE(y, yHat, ...)))
  }
  else if (output == "MAE"){
    return(c("MAE" = getMAE(y, yHat, ...)))
  }
  else if(output == "all"){
    RMSE = c("RMSE" = getRMSE(y, yHat, ...))
    MAE = c("MAE" = getMAE(y, yHat, ...))
    return(list(RMSE = RMSE, MAE = MAE))
  }
  else{
    stop("Bad Input")
  }
}




Generate Test Data

set.seed(10)
n <- 100
x <- runif(n)
resp <- 3 + 10 * x + rnorm(n)
pred <- predict(lm(resp ~ x), data.frame(x))

Test function
Both MAE and RMSE

getErrors(y = resp, yHat = pred)

$RMSE
     RMSE 
0.9581677 

$MAE
      MAE 
0.8155776 


MAE

getErrors(y = resp, yHat = pred, output = "MAE")

      MAE 
0.8155776 


RMSE

getErrors(y = resp, yHat = pred, output = "RMSE")

     RMSE 
0.9581677 


Remove Data & Test

resp[c(7,11)] = NA_real_

Test without NA behavior

getErrors(y = resp, yHat = pred, output = "RMSE")

RMSE 
  NA 


Test with NA Behavior

getErrors(y = resp, yHat = pred, output = "RMSE", na.rm = TRUE)

     RMSE 
0.9538186 


Test with poorly specified data:

getErrors(y = as.character(resp), yHat = pred, output = "RMSE", na.rm = TRUE)

Error in getErrors(y = as.character(resp), yHat = pred, output = "RMSE", : Bad input



getErrors(y = data.frame(resp), yHat = pred, output = "RMSE", na.rm = TRUE)

Error in getErrors(y = data.frame(resp), yHat = pred, output = "RMSE", : Bad input
